summary: I read `guides/backend-guide.md` and `guides/ui-style-guide.md`, then checked the full Dashboard-UI diff under review against them — the `Dashboard::ActivePipelines`/`RecentActivity`/`FleetHealth` query objects, the `Dashboard::Broadcast` service and its ~9 service-layer call sites, `HomeController`/`PhasesController`, the `status_helper` badge, the `home/*` views, the three Stimulus controllers, and the guide additions. The core architecture is strongly aligned: business logic sits in POROs, controllers are thin, broadcasts fire from services after the write to the smallest DOM unit via a per-user stream (progress ticks correctly opt out), the shared `status_badge` carries semantic tones with the word always shown, the presentation-boundary rescue is scoped to one read per panel, and — critically — both genuinely new patterns were documented in the guides in the same PR as CLAUDE.md requires. I found no blockers or majors, and confirmed two sibling code-quality issues (sweep unstuck asymmetry, manager_tick duplicate fan-out) are now fixed in the current tree. I emitted a `needs_work` verdict with six minor findings: the five minor deviations from the prior iteration are all still unaddressed in the current code (off-scale red/green shades, a hand-rolled badge bypassing `status_badge`, business logic in `define_helper`, empty states missing icon+action, and the fleet poll fallback lacking a sanctioning guide note), plus one new off-scale-spacing deviation (`mt-0.5`/`py-1.5`/`h-1.5`). Each is trivially fixable and none disturbs the sound architecture.
